Metadata.Tribunal_Court: General Division of the High Court of the Republic of Singapore

Party_Details.Name: Park Hotel Management Pte Ltd (in liquidation)

Facts.Text: Park Hotel Management Pte Ltd, a company incorporated in Singapore, was placed in liquidation on 2 July 2021.

On 25 February 2020, Mr Law wrote to Ascendas Hospitality Real Estate Investment Trust and New Park Property Limited to request a revision of the rent and other charges payable under the Park Hotel Clarke Quay and Grand Park Orchard leases, citing declining hotel occupancy rates and higher costs to implement safety measures due to the COVID-19 pandemic.

On 21 April 2020, Park Hotel CQ Pte Ltd and Grand Park OR Pte Ltd applied under the COVID-19 (Temporary Measures) Act 2020 for temporary relief from their contractual obligations under their respective leases.

Temporary relief under the COVID-19 (Temporary Measures) Act 2020 was granted to Park Hotel CQ Pte Ltd and Grand Park OR Pte Ltd until 19 November 2020, after which they were required to resume full payment of rent and other charges, pay monthly instalments for arrears under the Statutory Repayment Scheme, and top up their security deposits.

According to draft financial statements for the financial year ending 31 December 2020, Park Hotel CQ Pte Ltd had net current liabilities of S$18,964,276 and net total liabilities of S$4,654,826, and Grand Park OR Pte Ltd had net current liabilities of S$15,666,721 and net total liabilities of S$11,569,730.

Park Hotel Management Pte Ltd furnished Ascendas Hospitality Real Estate Investment Trust and New Park Property Limited each a guarantee and indemnity to secure the liabilities of Park Hotel CQ Pte Ltd and Grand Park OR Pte Ltd under their respective leases.

As a result of the COVID-19 outbreak, Singapore shut its borders on 23 March 2020. Visitor arrivals fell sharply and dining-in was prohibited during the Circuit Breaker measures, and the Park Hotel Group was affected.

On 11 September 2020, Grand Park OR Pte Ltd transferred S$1,000,000 to Park Hotel Management Pte Ltd's HSBC account, from which S$3,419,682.46 was transferred to the Laws' UBS Account on the same day.

Between 9 December 2020 and 4 January 2021, Park Hotel CQ Pte Ltd transferred a total of S$4,000,000 to Park Hotel Management Pte Ltd's UOB account, resulting in a balance of S$4,413,505.21, which was transferred on 8 January 2021 from that UOB account to the Laws' UBS Account.

On 24 December 2020, Grand Park OR Pte Ltd transferred S$1,998,681.69 to Park Hotel Management Pte Ltd's HSBC account, and on 9 January 2021 S$1,968,604.46 was transferred from that HSBC account to the Laws' UBS Account.

On 9 December 2020, Park Hotel CQ Pte Ltd and Grand Park OR Pte Ltd again applied for temporary relief under the COVID-19 (Temporary Measures) Act 2020 on the basis that their respective leases were tourism-related contracts, despite advice from Tan Kok Quan Partnership that the applications were unlikely to succeed.

On 8 March 2021, Park Hotel Management Pte Ltd entered into an Asset and Share Transfer Agreement with Park Hotel Group Management Pte Ltd, a Framework Agreement with Good Movement Holdings Limited, and a Business Transfer Agreement with SG Inst. Of Hospitality Pte Ltd.
On 23 March 2021, Park Hotel Management Pte Ltd entered into a Trademark Assignment Agreement with Park Hotel Group Management Pte Ltd.
On 1 March 2021, before the Framework Agreement was signed, Mr Law procured PHMPL to transfer its shares in Yan Pte Ltd to GMHL.
On or around 3 March 2021, Mr Law procured PHMPL to declare the S$22m Dividend but back-dated this to 1 September 2020.

On 12 March 2021, Mr Law caused PHMPL to draw down S$5,000,000 from its UOB loan facility, which was paid into PHMPL's UOB account, such that it had a credit of S$6,700,984.09; on the same day, S$6,698,130.81 was transferred from PHMPL's UOB account to the Laws' UBS Account.
On 6 April 2021, Mr Law approved the transfer of S$1,054,089.06 from PHMPL's HSBC account to his personal UOB account.
On 5 April 2021, Mr Law procured PHMPL to declare the S$5.9m Dividend but back-dated this to 23 March 2021.

In March 2021, Mr Law announced to PHMPL's employees that there would be a restructuring and new entities would be formed, and that all employees would be transferred to the new entity accordingly effectively in April, with no change in employment arrangements apart from the legal employer.

Mr Ng's employment with PHMPL officially ceased on 31 March 2021 and his employment with PHGM began on 1 April 2021 on identical terms; however, his letter of cessation of employment with PHMPL and letter of employment with PHGM were only prepared and signed in May 2021.

On 31 March 2021, New Park Property Limited sent letters of demand to Grand Park OR Pte Ltd and Park Hotel Management Pte Ltd, and on 5 April 2021 PHMPL stated that it would not be making payment of the sums claimed by NPP in respect of the GPOR Lease on the grounds of frustration.
On 7 June 2021, New Park Property Limited filed an application to wind up Park Hotel Management Pte Ltd.

On or around 29 June 2021, Mr Ng testified that he was instructed by Ms Tang to create the Journal Entries, which were dated 30 November 2020 and had the effect of increasing PHMPL's liability to Mr Law by S$6,750,000.
By the middle of March 2021, Mr Law had reduced PHMPL to a shell.

On 4 March 2021, Mr Law and Ms Tang discussed by e-mail whether adding a line to an amendment agreement with ART would help the frustration case later and whether they needed to negotiate with GPOR's landlord to buy time.
On 1 March 2021, Ms Tang informed Mr Law that PHMPL's disposal of Yan Pte Ltd was a substantial disposal required to be lodged with ACRA, and Mr Law replied '[l]ater the better'.
On 2 March 2021, Mr Law instructed Ms Tang to lodge the change of directors of PHCQ and GPOR only after all the Contracts had been transferred out of PHMPL.
On 16 March 2021, Mr Law appointed Mr Lim, the general manager of both Park Hotel Clarke Quay and Grand Park Orchard hotels, to replace him as director of PHCQ and GPOR.

Mr Law instructed for the ownership of PHGM to be transferred to GMHL, and he instructed for the ownership of Yan Pte Ltd to be transferred to GMHL and for the SIH business to be transferred to SIOHPL.
On 4 March 2021, Ms Tang prepared and sent the computation for the S$200,000 consideration under the BTA to Mr Law, and he approved it within minutes after receipt.

On 3 March 2021, after Mr Law had signed the director's resolution declaring the S$22m Dividend, Ms Tang wrote to Mr Ng, 'Please do the necessary and re run Navision. Discussion on how your proposal to clear the balances'.

On 10 August 2017, Ms Tang sent an email to Mr Law outlining a proposal for a group restructuring that envisaged a single ultimate holding company and PHMPL remaining as the intermediate holding company for the hotel management business line.

Party_Details.Issue: Whether pre-dispute transfers of Park Hotel Management Pte Ltd's assets, businesses, and cash to companies owned or controlled by Mr Law, and related internal accounting entries and dividends, resulted in a depletion of assets available to meet liabilities, including exposure under corporate guarantees for subsidiary lease obligations.
Party_Details.Rule: Under company and insolvency law, transactions such as asset transfers, dividends, and set-offs may be set aside or give rise to liability if they occur when a company is unable to pay its debts as they fall due or becomes unable to do so as a result, and if they involve dispositions at an undervalue or preferential treatment of certain creditors or related parties compared to other creditors.
Party_Details.Application: Park Hotel Management Pte Ltd operated hotel management, food and beverage, and training businesses and held hotel management agreements, trademarks, and shares in subsidiaries. Its subsidiaries Park Hotel CQ Pte Ltd and Grand Park OR Pte Ltd stopped paying rent in 2020, applied for statutory relief, and remained in arrears after relief expired, while New Park Property Limited drew down on security deposits and issued a letter of demand on 20 February 2021. Park Hotel Management Pte Ltd had given corporate guarantees for these leases. After the 20 February 2021 demand, Mr Law and Ms Tang discussed proceeding quickly with a restructuring and minimising the risk of challenge by landlords. Between late February and March 2021, Park Hotel Management Pte Ltd entered into agreements to transfer hotel management contracts, trademarks, intellectual property, business information, shares in Park Hotel Affiliates Pte Ltd, shares in Park Hotel Management (Maldives) Pvt Ltd, shares in Yan Pte Ltd, and the Singapore Institute of Hospitality business to Park Hotel Group Management Pte Ltd, Good Movement Holdings Limited, and SG Inst. Of Hospitality Pte Ltd, all ultimately owned or controlled by Mr Law. During 2020 and early 2021, cash from Park Hotel CQ Pte Ltd and Grand Park OR Pte Ltd was routed through Park Hotel Management Pte Ltd to a UBS account held by Mr Law and Ms Tan, and Park Hotel Management Pte Ltd drew down S$5,000,000 from its UOB facility and transferred almost the entire amount to the same UBS account. Park Hotel Management Pte Ltd recorded large interim dividends and journal entries in its ledgers, dated to earlier periods, and used these entries to offset amounts recorded as owing by Mr Law and his related entities, including the consideration under the asset transfer agreements and prior cash transfers. Employees and operational infrastructure were moved from Park Hotel Management Pte Ltd to Park Hotel Group Management Pte Ltd while Park Hotel Management Pte Ltd remained exposed under the corporate guarantees and faced a winding-up application filed on 7 June 2021, leading to its liquidation on 2 July 2021.
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The judgment describes Park Hotel Management Pte Ltd as an international hotel operator whose financial position deteriorated after COVID-19, with its subsidiaries Park Hotel CQ Pte Ltd and Grand Park OR Pte Ltd defaulting on lease payments. The judge finds that by December 2020, and at the latest by 20 February 2021 when New Park Property Limited issued a demand, Park Hotel Management Pte Ltd was unable to pay its debts as they fell due once proper allowance was made for its exposure under corporate guarantees and impairment of receivables. The court concludes that Mr Law orchestrated a restructuring to move all viable, revenue-generating assets and businesses from Park Hotel Management Pte Ltd to companies he owned or controlled, leaving Park Hotel Management Pte Ltd as a shell with substantial liabilities. The judge relies on contemporaneous emails and internal documents to find that Mr Law and Ms Tang acted with urgency after the 20 February 2021 demand, discussed minimising the risk of challenge and buying time from landlords, and sought to shield the new structure from third-party claims. The court finds that the Asset and Share Transfer Agreement, Framework Agreement, Business Transfer Agreement, and Trademark Assignment Agreement were not negotiated at arm's length, that Mr Law effectively decided the consideration on both sides without independent valuation, and that the consideration was significantly below the true value of the transferred assets. The judge accepts evidence that Park Hotel Management Pte Ltd's employees, IT systems, and operational infrastructure were moved to Park Hotel Group Management Pte Ltd, while Park Hotel Management Pte Ltd remained liable under the corporate guarantees. The judgment characterises the S$22 million and S$5.9 million dividends and the S$6.75 million journal entries as devices to eliminate receivables owed by Mr Law and his entities to Park Hotel Management Pte Ltd, noting that these entries were backdated and created after the company was insolvent and facing winding up. The court rejects explanations that backdating was routine and finds that the timing and structure of the entries were intended to give an appearance of legitimacy and to hinder creditor recovery. The judge concludes that the asset transfers, dividends, cash extractions, and set-offs were transactions at an undervalue and unfair preferences under the Insolvency, Restructuring and Dissolution Act, and that Mr Law breached his duties as director by prioritising his own interests and those of the defendant companies over those of Park Hotel Management Pte Ltd and its creditors. The court further finds that the defendant companies, being owned or controlled by Mr Law and benefiting from the transfers, are liable for accessory claims such as dishonest assistance, knowing receipt, and conspiracy. The final orders include setting aside or granting relief in respect of the impugned transactions, directing repayment of specified cash sums and receivables, and holding Mr Law and the defendant companies liable, with detailed quantification in the annexes.

Metadata.Tribunal_Court: General Division of the High Court of the Republic of Singapore

Party_Details.Name: Aw Eng Hai

Facts.Text: Aw Eng Hai is one of the liquidators of Park Hotel Management Pte Ltd.
Aw Eng Hai is a licensed liquidator and chartered accountant.

Party_Details.Issue: Whether the financial analysis conducted by the liquidator, including adjustments for impaired receivables from subsidiaries and liabilities under corporate guarantees, showed that Park Hotel Management Pte Ltd was unable to pay its debts as they fell due before the impugned transactions.
Party_Details.Rule: In assessing a company's solvency under the cash flow test, a liquidator may adjust the balance sheet to reflect realistic recoverability of receivables and contingent liabilities that are likely to be demanded, including obligations under guarantees, to determine whether current assets exceed current liabilities over a reasonable period.
Party_Details.Application: As liquidator, Aw Eng Hai examined Park Hotel Management Pte Ltd's draft financial statements for the year ending 31 December 2020 and identified that receivables from Park Hotel CQ Pte Ltd and Grand Park OR Pte Ltd were unlikely to be recoverable given their net liability positions and persistent rental arrears. He also considered that Park Hotel Management Pte Ltd had issued corporate guarantees for the subsidiaries' leases and that demands under these guarantees were likely once landlords exhausted security deposits and temporary relief expired. By adjusting the balance sheet to impair receivables from the subsidiaries and to recognise expected liabilities under the guarantees, he concluded that Park Hotel Management Pte Ltd's current liabilities exceeded its current assets and that it could not meet its debts as they fell due by the end of 2020 or, at the latest, by February 2021.
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The court accepts the liquidator's methodology of adjusting Park Hotel Management Pte Ltd's balance sheet to reflect the practical unrecoverability of receivables from insolvent subsidiaries and the realistic exposure under corporate guarantees. The judge notes that Park Hotel CQ Pte Ltd and Grand Park OR Pte Ltd had substantial net current and total liabilities and had ceased paying rent, and that landlords had drawn down security deposits and issued demands. The judgment endorses the liquidator's view that the company's recorded current assets were overstated and its current liabilities understated, and that once corrected, the company failed the cash flow test by December 2020. This analysis supports the finding that the company was insolvent when the asset transfers, dividends, and set-offs occurred, satisfying the statutory requirements for transactions at an undervalue and unfair preferences and underpinning liability findings against Mr Law and the defendant companies.

Metadata.Tribunal_Court: General Division of the High Court of the Republic of Singapore

Party_Details.Name: Kon Yin Tong

Facts.Text: Kon Yin Tong is one of the liquidators of Park Hotel Management Pte Ltd.

Party_Details.Issue: Whether, as a joint liquidator, Kon Yin Tong participates in the administration and recovery actions arising from pre-liquidation transactions affecting Park Hotel Management Pte Ltd's asset base.
Party_Details.Rule: Joint liquidators appointed upon a company's winding up are responsible for realising assets and pursuing claims to recover value lost through pre-liquidation transactions that may be set aside under insolvency and company law.
Party_Details.Application: Upon the winding up of Park Hotel Management Pte Ltd on 2 July 2021, Kon Yin Tong, together with the other liquidator, assumed responsibility for investigating the company's affairs, including asset transfers, dividends, and set-offs executed before liquidation, and for bringing proceedings to recover value for creditors where transactions are found to be avoidable or to give rise to director or third-party liability.
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The judgment identifies Kon Yin Tong as a co-liquidator of Park Hotel Management Pte Ltd but does not attribute separate conduct or fault to him. His role is treated as part of the liquidators' collective function in bringing the action. The court's liability findings are directed at Mr Law and the defendant companies, not at the liquidators, and the reference to Kon Yin Tong serves to establish standing to pursue the claims and to implement the court's orders for recovery.

Metadata.Tribunal_Court: General Division of the High Court of the Republic of Singapore

Party_Details.Name: Law Ching Hung

Facts.Text: From at least 2017 until March 2021, Law Ching Hung was the sole shareholder and director of Park Hotel Management Pte Ltd.
During the material period, Law Ching Hung was the sole shareholder and director of Good Movement Holdings Limited, a company incorporated in the British Virgin Islands.
During the material period, Law Ching Hung was a director of Park Hotel CQ Pte Ltd and Grand Park OR Pte Ltd until 16 March 2021.
During the material period, Law Ching Hung was married to Ms Tan Shin Hui, who later became the sole director of Park Hotel Group Management Pte Ltd.

During the material period, Law Ching Hung was the sole shareholder and director of Good Movement Holdings Limited, which owned Park Hotel Group Management Pte Ltd and SG Inst. Of Hospitality Pte Ltd.

On 25 February 2020, Law Ching Hung wrote to Ascendas Hospitality Real Estate Investment Trust and New Park Property Limited requesting a revision of rent and other charges under the Park Hotel Clarke Quay and Grand Park Orchard leases, referring to declining occupancy and increased safety costs.

On 6 March 2020, in correspondence with Tan Kok Quan Partnership, Law Ching Hung referred to a corporate guarantee by Park Hotel Management Pte Ltd and stated that the group could not afford to go into default under the leases.

On 21 April 2020, in applications under the COVID-19 (Temporary Measures) Act 2020 for Park Hotel CQ Pte Ltd and Grand Park OR Pte Ltd, Law Ching Hung stated that the companies were unable to pay rent from March and April 2020 respectively and had experienced a large negative cash flow since February 2020.

On 9 December 2020, despite advice from Tan Kok Quan Partnership that further applications were unlikely to succeed, Mr Law caused PHCQ and GPOR to apply again for temporary relief under the COVID-19 (Temporary Measures) Act 2020 on the basis that their leases were tourism-related contracts.

On 11 September 2020, after Grand Park OR Pte Ltd transferred S$1,000,000 to Park Hotel Management Pte Ltd's HSBC account, S$3,419,682.46 was transferred from that account to a UBS account held jointly by Law Ching Hung and Ms Tan.
On 8 January 2021, S$4,413,505.21 was transferred from PHMPL's UOB account, which had received S$4,000,000 from PHCQ, to the Laws' UBS Account.
On 9 January 2021, S$1,968,604.46 was transferred from PHMPL's HSBC account, which had received S$1,998,681.69 from GPOR, to the Laws' UBS Account.

On 23 February 2021, Mr Law appointed lawyers from Bryan Cave Leighton Paisner LLP to prepare the relevant agreements to dispose of PHMPL's assets and informed them of the Corporate Guarantees and the need to ensure the restructuring exercise would shield the new setup from any third-party claims on PHMPL.

On 1 March 2021, before the Framework Agreement was signed, Law Ching Hung signed a share transfer form to transfer Park Hotel Management Pte Ltd's shares in Yan Pte Ltd to Good Movement Holdings Limited and left the date to be inserted by staff.

On 2 March 2021, after receiving a list of Park Hotel Management Pte Ltd's assets and businesses from Ms Tang, Law Ching Hung selected which assets and businesses would be transferred to companies owned by Good Movement Holdings Limited, emphasising that trademarks and hotel management contracts must be transferred.
On 2 March 2021, Mr Law instructed that PHGM's ownership be transferred to GMHL and that Ms Tan replace him as director of PHGM, with effect targeted for 1 March 2021.

On 3 March 2021, Mr Law approved a computation prepared by Ms Tang that set the consideration of S$2,700,000 for the transfer of hotel management contracts and related assets from PHMPL to PHGM under the ASTA.

On or around 3 March 2021, Mr Law procured PHMPL to declare the S$22m Dividend but back-dated this to 1 September 2020, and it was used to eliminate various debts owed to PHMPL, including amounts owed by GMHL, Grand Park Maldives and Mr Law.

On 3 March 2021, after Mr Law had signed the director's resolution declaring the S$22m Dividend, Ms Tang wrote to Mr Ng, 'Please do the necessary and re run Navision. Discussion on how your proposal to clear the balances'.

On 5 March 2021, in an e-mail response to Ms Tang's e-mail containing the draft board and shareholder resolutions for the restructuring, Mr Law asked, 'any benefits in calling it [a] restructuring exercise? Why not just arm's length [sale] and purchase ?' and also questioned Ms Tang's use of the word 'reorganisation' in the draft of the BTA.
On 5 March 2021, in relation to the draft Framework Agreement, Mr Law asked whether his name could be removed, and the transfer of PHM (HK) was removed from the Framework Agreement.
On 6 March 2021, in response to BCLP's query about removing references to the internal restructuring, Mr Law told BCLP that this was 'more like a Sale and Purchase between the various entities'.
On or around 8 March 2021, Mr Law arranged for Ms Tan to sign the ASTA and for Ms Soh to sign the Framework Agreement on behalf of GMHL, even though Ms Soh did not hold a formal role in GMHL.

On 12 March 2021, Mr Law caused PHMPL to draw down S$5,000,000 from its UOB loan facility and transfer S$6,698,130.81 from PHMPL's UOB account to the Laws' UBS Account, leaving PHMPL's UOB account with only S$2,853.28.
On 16 March 2021, Mr Law appointed Mr Lim as director of both PHCQ and GPOR; Mr Law continued to manage both companies.
On 23 March 2021, PHMPL agreed under the TMAA to assign 135 trademarks to PHGM for the consideration of S$1.
On 5 April 2021, Mr Law instructed Ms Tang to (a) clear his director's account with PHMPL; (b) pay the balance sum to him; and (c) pay UOB any cash that PHMPL, GPOR or PHCQ may have left.
On 6 April 2021, Mr Law approved the transfer of S$1,054,089.06 from PHMPL's HSBC account to his personal UOB account.

In April and May 2021, while PHCQ and PHMPL were in arrears to ART, Mr Law engaged in discussions with ART about repayment plans while PHMPL had already transferred its hotel management business and other assets to companies owned by GMHL.

On 10 June 2021, after the winding up application against PHMPL had been filed, Ms Tang wrote to the owner of Destination Singapore Beach Road hotel (copying Mr Law) seeking consent for PHMPL to novate the right to the HMA entered with the hotel and proposed to waive the management fees due to PHMPL in March 2021 as a gesture of goodwill.

Party_Details.Issue: Whether, before the commencement of the dispute, Law Ching Hung's control over Park Hotel Management Pte Ltd and related entities, his decisions to transfer assets and businesses to companies he owned or controlled, and his directions regarding cash movements and internal accounting entries, resulted in the removal of value from Park Hotel Management Pte Ltd while it faced significant lease-related liabilities and exposure under corporate guarantees.
Party_Details.Rule: Company directors who control both a company and its counterparties must ensure that transactions, including asset transfers, dividends, and set-offs, are conducted on proper terms, particularly when the company is or may become unable to pay its debts as they fall due. Transactions that move value to related parties at inadequate consideration or that clear related-party debts through internal entries when the company is in financial difficulty may be treated as transactions at an undervalue or unfair preferences under insolvency law and may give rise to personal liability.
Party_Details.Application: As sole shareholder and director of Park Hotel Management Pte Ltd and of Good Movement Holdings Limited, and as director of Park Hotel CQ Pte Ltd and Grand Park OR Pte Ltd until mid-March 2021, Law Ching Hung controlled the key entities in the Park Hotel group. In 2020, he acknowledged in correspondence with lawyers that Park Hotel Management Pte Ltd had given corporate guarantees for the subsidiaries' leases and that default under those leases would expose the guarantor. After the subsidiaries ceased paying rent and temporary statutory relief expired, landlords drew down security deposits and issued a letter of demand on 20 February 2021. Within two days, Law Ching Hung initiated a restructuring, engaged external counsel to prepare transfer agreements, and instructed that the new structure be shielded from third-party claims on Park Hotel Management Pte Ltd. He selected which assets and businesses would be moved, ensured that Park Hotel Group Management Pte Ltd and SG Inst. Of Hospitality Pte Ltd were owned by Good Movement Holdings Limited, and arranged for his spouse and an employee to be the recorded directors of those companies while he remained the ultimate owner. He approved consideration figures for the Asset and Share Transfer Agreement, Framework Agreement, and Business Transfer Agreement based on internal computations without independent valuation, including using net asset value for Yan Pte Ltd after arranging for that company to declare a dividend that reduced its net asset value. He signed a share transfer form for Yan Pte Ltd before the Framework Agreement was executed. During the same period, cash from Park Hotel CQ Pte Ltd and Grand Park OR Pte Ltd was transferred through Park Hotel Management Pte Ltd to a UBS account held jointly by him and Ms Tan, and Park Hotel Management Pte Ltd drew down S$5,000,000 from its UOB facility and transferred almost the entire amount to the same UBS account. Law Ching Hung signed resolutions for Park Hotel Management Pte Ltd declaring large interim dividends and instructed that these and later journal entries be used to clear his director's loan account and related-party balances, including the consideration under the transfer agreements and prior cash transfers. He directed that any remaining cash in Park Hotel Management Pte Ltd, Park Hotel CQ Pte Ltd, or Grand Park OR Pte Ltd be used to pay United Overseas Bank, whose facility he had personally guaranteed. He also arranged for employees and operational infrastructure to move from Park Hotel Management Pte Ltd to Park Hotel Group Management Pte Ltd while Park Hotel Management Pte Ltd remained exposed under the corporate guarantees and faced a winding-up application. These pre-dispute actions he used his control over the group to move assets and cash to entities he owned or controlled and to adjust internal accounts in a way that reduced amounts recorded as owing by him and his entities to Park Hotel Management Pte Ltd at a time when the company faced significant lease-related liabilities.
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The court finds that Law Ching Hung was the central decision-maker in the Park Hotel group and that he used his control to prioritise his own interests and those of companies he owned or controlled over the interests of Park Hotel Management Pte Ltd and its creditors. The judge concludes that he breached his duties as director by orchestrating a restructuring that transferred all viable, revenue-generating assets and businesses out of Park Hotel Management Pte Ltd at a time when the company was insolvent or on the verge of insolvency, and by causing the company to enter into transactions at an undervalue and to give unfair preferences under the Insolvency, Restructuring and Dissolution Act. The judgment emphasises that the Asset and Share Transfer Agreement, Framework Agreement, Business Transfer Agreement, and Trademark Assignment Agreement were not negotiated at arm's length, that consideration was depressed through methods such as using high expense ratios and net asset value after dividends, and that no independent valuation was obtained. The court finds that large interim dividends and journal entries were backdated and used to eliminate receivables owed by Law Ching Hung and his entities, including the consideration under the transfer agreements and prior cash transfers, and that these entries were made when Park Hotel Management Pte Ltd was insolvent and facing winding up. The judge rejects explanations that backdating and set-offs were routine and concludes that they were intended to hinder or delay creditor claims. The court also notes that Law Ching Hung sought to conceal his role in the restructuring by removing references to himself and to an internal reorganisation from transaction documents and by arranging for others to be recorded as directors or signatories. The judgment holds him personally liable for breach of duty, for transactions at an undervalue and unfair preferences, and for related accessory and conspiracy claims, and orders him to repay specified sums and to account for benefits obtained through the impugned transactions.

Metadata.Tribunal_Court: General Division of the High Court of the Republic of Singapore

Party_Details.Name: Park Hotel Group Management Pte Ltd

Facts.Text: Park Hotel Group Management Pte Ltd is a company incorporated in Singapore in 2017 and was formerly known as Park Baobab Pte Ltd.

During the material period, Park Hotel Group Management Pte Ltd's sole director was Ms Tan Shin Hui, and it was owned by Good Movement Holdings Limited, which was wholly owned and controlled by Law Ching Hung.

Party_Details.Issue: Whether, before the dispute, Park Hotel Group Management Pte Ltd received hotel management contracts, trademarks, shares, and operational staff from Park Hotel Management Pte Ltd on terms that removed value from Park Hotel Management Pte Ltd while Park Hotel Group Management Pte Ltd was owned and controlled through Good Movement Holdings Limited by the same individual who controlled Park Hotel Management Pte Ltd.
Party_Details.Rule: A company that receives assets, contracts, or business operations from another company at a time when the transferor is insolvent or close to insolvency, and where the recipient is owned or controlled by the same person who controls the transferor, may be claims under insolvency and trust principles if the transfers are at an undervalue or constitute preferences, particularly where the recipient benefits from the continuation of the business without assuming corresponding liabilities.
Party_Details.Application: Park Hotel Group Management Pte Ltd was incorporated in 2017 and, by early March 2021, was owned by Good Movement Holdings Limited and had Ms Tan as its sole director, while Good Movement Holdings Limited was wholly owned and controlled by Law Ching Hung. On 8 March 2021, Park Hotel Group Management Pte Ltd entered into an Asset and Share Transfer Agreement with Park Hotel Management Pte Ltd to acquire multiple hotel management agreements, trademark licence agreements, licence agreements, business records, business names, intellectual property rights, IT systems, insurance benefits, and shares in Park Hotel Affiliates Pte Ltd and Park Hotel Management (Maldives) Pvt Ltd for a stated consideration of S$2,700,000 and US$39,600. On 23 March 2021, it entered into a Trademark Assignment Agreement to acquire 135 trademarks for S$1. Internal communications the consideration for these transfers was set by computations prepared by Ms Tang and approved by Law Ching Hung, without independent valuation, and that Park Hotel Group Management Pte Ltd did not assume Park Hotel Management Pte Ltd's liabilities associated with the transferred assets. From 1 April 2021, Park Hotel Group Management Pte Ltd employed staff previously employed by Park Hotel Management Pte Ltd and used the transferred IT systems and business infrastructure to continue the hotel management business. These pre-dispute events Park Hotel Group Management Pte Ltd became the vehicle through which the hotel management business and related assets of Park Hotel Management Pte Ltd were continued, while Park Hotel Management Pte Ltd was left without those assets and remained exposed to lease-related liabilities and corporate guarantees.
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The court finds that Park Hotel Group Management Pte Ltd was effectively a recipient of Park Hotel Management Pte Ltd's hotel management business, trademarks, and related assets under transactions orchestrated by Law Ching Hung when Park Hotel Management Pte Ltd was insolvent or nearly insolvent. The judge concludes that the consideration paid by Park Hotel Group Management Pte Ltd under the Asset and Share Transfer Agreement and the Trademark Assignment Agreement was significantly below the true value of the assets, based on expert valuation evidence and the manner in which the consideration was calculated. The judgment notes that Park Hotel Group Management Pte Ltd did not assume corresponding liabilities and that the business continued seamlessly under its ownership with the same staff and management. The court holds that Park Hotel Group Management Pte Ltd is liable for knowing receipt of assets transferred in breach of duty and for dishonest assistance in Law Ching Hung's breaches, given its ownership and control structure and the involvement of its director and finance personnel in implementing the transfers. The company is also found to have participated in an unlawful means conspiracy with Law Ching Hung and the other defendant companies to strip Park Hotel Management Pte Ltd of its assets and to defeat creditor claims. Orders are made requiring Park Hotel Group Management Pte Ltd to account for and restore value corresponding to the assets and business it received.

Metadata.Tribunal_Court: General Division of the High Court of the Republic of Singapore

Party_Details.Name: Good Movement Holdings Limited

Facts.Text: Under the Framework Agreement dated 8 March 2021, PHMPL agreed to transfer to GMHL 500,000 ordinary shares (the entire issued share capital) of Yan Pte Ltd for S$500,000 and 10 ordinary shares (the remaining 1% of the issued share capital) of Park Hotel Maldives for US$400.
On 1 March 2021, Mr Law procured PHMPL to transfer its shares in Yan Pte Ltd to GMHL before the Framework Agreement had been signed.

On 3 March 2021, Yan Pte Ltd declared a dividend of S$904,362.49, which was back-dated to 31 January 2021. This was subsequently changed to S$891,165.60, which had the effect of reducing the net asset value and therefore the consideration under the Framework Agreement to exactly S$500,000.

Party_Details.Issue: Whether, before the dispute, Good Movement Holdings Limited, owned and controlled by the same individual who controlled Park Hotel Management Pte Ltd, received shares in Yan Pte Ltd and Park Hotel Management (Maldives) Pvt Ltd and benefited from loans and asset transfers recorded in Park Hotel Management Pte Ltd's books at values and on terms that reduced Park Hotel Management Pte Ltd's asset base while it faced significant liabilities.
Party_Details.Rule: A holding company that receives shares or benefits from loans and asset transfers from an insolvent or near-insolvent company controlled by the same individual may be recovery actions under insolvency and trust law where the transfers are at an undervalue or constitute preferences, particularly when internal accounting entries are used to clear intercompany balances without corresponding cash settlement.
Party_Details.Application: Good Movement Holdings Limited, incorporated in the British Virgin Islands and wholly owned and directed by Law Ching Hung, owned Park Hotel Group Management Pte Ltd and SG Inst. Of Hospitality Pte Ltd. On 11 September 2020, S$3,419,682.46 transferred from Park Hotel Management Pte Ltd to a UBS account held by Law Ching Hung and Ms Tan was recorded in Park Hotel Management Pte Ltd's books as a loan to Good Movement Holdings Limited described as an investment in a Kyoto Oike project. On 1 March 2021, before the Framework Agreement was executed, Park Hotel Management Pte Ltd executed a share transfer form transferring its shares in Yan Pte Ltd to Good Movement Holdings Limited. On 3 March 2021, Yan Pte Ltd declared a dividend backdated to 31 January 2021 that reduced its net asset value, and the Framework Agreement signed on 8 March 2021 set the consideration for Yan Pte Ltd at S$500,000, matching the reduced net asset value. The Framework Agreement also provided for Good Movement Holdings Limited to acquire the remaining 1% of Park Hotel Management (Maldives) Pvt Ltd for US$400. Internal accounting entries in Park Hotel Management Pte Ltd's ledgers later used large interim dividends and journal entries to clear amounts recorded as owing by Good Movement Holdings Limited, including the S$3,419,682.46 loan, without evidence of corresponding cash repayment. These pre-dispute events Good Movement Holdings Limited received shares and the benefit of loans and transfers from Park Hotel Management Pte Ltd while being owned and controlled by the same individual who directed the transferor, at a time when Park Hotel Management Pte Ltd faced significant lease-related liabilities and exposure under corporate guarantees.
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The court finds that Good Movement Holdings Limited was a key vehicle through which Law Ching Hung moved value out of Park Hotel Management Pte Ltd. The judgment notes that a substantial cash transfer from Park Hotel Management Pte Ltd to a UBS account held by Law Ching Hung and Ms Tan was recorded as a loan to Good Movement Holdings Limited and that this receivable was later eliminated through the use of a backdated S$22 million dividend and set-offs. The court concludes that the acquisition of Yan Pte Ltd and the remaining shares in Park Hotel Management (Maldives) Pvt Ltd by Good Movement Holdings Limited under the Framework Agreement occurred at an undervalue, given the way net asset value was reduced by a backdated dividend and the absence of independent valuation. The judge holds that Good Movement Holdings Limited, being wholly owned and directed by Law Ching Hung and benefiting from these transactions, is liable for knowing receipt and dishonest assistance in relation to the breaches of duty by Law Ching Hung and for participation in an unlawful means conspiracy to strip Park Hotel Management Pte Ltd of its assets. The company is ordered to restore value corresponding to the benefits it received, as detailed in the annexes to the judgment.

Metadata.Tribunal_Court: General Division of the High Court of the Republic of Singapore

Party_Details.Name: Sg. Inst. Of Hospitality Pte Ltd

Facts.Text: Sg. Inst. Of Hospitality Pte Ltd was incorporated in Singapore on 4 March 2021. It was owned by Good Movement Holdings Limited, and its sole director was Ms Juliana Soh.

On 8 March 2021, Sg. Inst. Of Hospitality Pte Ltd entered into a Business Transfer Agreement with Park Hotel Management Pte Ltd under which it agreed to acquire all businesses carried on by Park Hotel Management Pte Ltd under the trade name Singapore Institute of Hospitality and all fixed and current assets owned by Park Hotel Management Pte Ltd for the purposes of that business, for a stated consideration of S$200,000.
On 4 March 2021, Ms Tang sent Law Ching Hung a computation for the S$200,000 consideration under the Business Transfer Agreement, and he approved it within minutes.
On or around 8 March 2021, Ms Soh signed the Business Transfer Agreement on behalf of Sg. Inst. Of Hospitality Pte Ltd.

Party_Details.Issue: Whether, before the dispute, Sg. Inst. Of Hospitality Pte Ltd, owned through Good Movement Holdings Limited by the same individual who controlled Park Hotel Management Pte Ltd, received the Singapore Institute of Hospitality business and related assets from Park Hotel Management Pte Ltd for a consideration determined internally without independent valuation, at a time when Park Hotel Management Pte Ltd faced significant liabilities.
Party_Details.Rule: A newly incorporated company that acquires a business and assets from an insolvent or near-insolvent company controlled by the same individual, for consideration set by internal computations without independent valuation and without assuming corresponding liabilities, may be recovery actions under insolvency and trust law if the transfer is at an undervalue or constitutes a preference.
Party_Details.Application: Sg. Inst. Of Hospitality Pte Ltd was incorporated on 4 March 2021 and was owned by Good Movement Holdings Limited, which was wholly owned and directed by Law Ching Hung. Its sole director was Ms Juliana Soh, who had previously managed the Singapore Institute of Hospitality business within Park Hotel Management Pte Ltd. On 4 March 2021, Ms Tang prepared and sent to Law Ching Hung a computation valuing the Singapore Institute of Hospitality business at S$200,000, based on conservative revenue assumptions that did not include projected new contracts or growth, and Law Ching Hung approved this computation within minutes. On 8 March 2021, Sg. Inst. Of Hospitality Pte Ltd entered into a Business Transfer Agreement with Park Hotel Management Pte Ltd to acquire the Singapore Institute of Hospitality business and all related fixed and current assets for S$200,000. The executed agreement did not include a clause transferring liabilities from Park Hotel Management Pte Ltd to Sg. Inst. Of Hospitality Pte Ltd, after Law Ching Hung had instructed that such a clause be removed from earlier drafts. Ms Soh signed the agreement on behalf of Sg. Inst. Of Hospitality Pte Ltd without negotiating the consideration or other key terms. These pre-dispute events Sg. Inst. Of Hospitality Pte Ltd received a business and assets from Park Hotel Management Pte Ltd for a consideration determined internally by individuals who also controlled the transferor, at a time when Park Hotel Management Pte Ltd faced significant lease-related liabilities and exposure under corporate guarantees.
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The court finds that Sg. Inst. Of Hospitality Pte Ltd was created as part of the restructuring to receive the Singapore Institute of Hospitality business from Park Hotel Management Pte Ltd. The judgment notes that the S$200,000 consideration was based on a computation prepared by Ms Tang and approved by Law Ching Hung, which used conservative assumptions and ignored more optimistic revenue forecasts provided by Ms Soh. The court concludes that the valuation was artificially low and that the Business Transfer Agreement was at an undervalue, particularly as Sg. Inst. Of Hospitality Pte Ltd did not assume the liabilities associated with the business. The judge finds that Ms Soh left the determination of consideration and key terms to Law Ching Hung and Ms Tang and that Sg. Inst. Of Hospitality Pte Ltd, being owned by Good Movement Holdings Limited and benefiting from the transfer, is liable for knowing receipt and dishonest assistance in relation to the breaches of duty by Law Ching Hung and for participation in an unlawful means conspiracy. The company is ordered to restore value corresponding to the business and assets it received.
